reforms.description: The Estonian government has introduced development vouchers to incentivize cooperation between research institutions and businesses, aiming to strengthen knowledge transfer and applied research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitates collaboration between academia and industry, enhancing innovation capacity and applied research outcomes.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has established development vouchers to promote cooperation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant step towards enhancing collaboration between research and business sectors, although it is not a comprehensive overhaul of the innovation system.
reforms.source_quote: Development vouchers incentivising co-operation between research institutions and businesses have been introduced in 2015.

reforms.description: The Estonian government is working on establishing a credit information-sharing scheme to improve access to credit for SMEs and startups, which is expected to enhance the overall financing landscape.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improves credit access for innovative firms, thereby fostering entrepreneurship and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is working on establishing a credit information-sharing scheme.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant for improving the financing environment for SMEs, although it does not represent a complete overhaul of the financial system.
reforms.source_quote: A well-designed credit information-sharing scheme should be established.

reforms.description: Estonia is enhancing the effectiveness of innovation policies by incorporating monitoring and evaluation at the design stage, which includes pilot projects for individual instruments.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving the evaluation of innovation policies can lead to more effective resource allocation and better outcomes for innovation initiatives.
reforms.status_evidence: Evaluations have been taken into account in the design of measures to support innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant improvement in the governance of innovation policies, which can enhance their effectiveness and impact on the economy.
reforms.source_quote: Enhance effectiveness of evaluation of innovation policies by incorporating monitoring and evaluation already at the design stage.

reforms.description: Estonia aims to improve its innovative capacity and knowledge transfer from highly productive firms to the rest of the economy, focusing on strengthening adult education and cooperation between businesses and researchers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing knowledge transfer is expected to raise productivity and innovation across the economy, leading to stronger economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Efforts should concentrate on strengthening adult education and cooperation between businesses and researchers.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to enhance collaboration between the private sector and research institutions, which is crucial for improving innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: Efforts should concentrate on strengthening adult education, immigration of talents, and cooperation between businesses and researchers.

reforms.description: The Estonian government plans to increase the weight of business contracts in the funding formula for public research institutions starting September 2017, aiming to enhance collaboration between public research and the private sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering collaboration between public research institutions and the private sector, the reform is expected to enhance innovation and productivity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The weight of business contracts in the funding formula will be increased as of September 2017.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how public research funding is allocated, emphasizing the importance of private sector collaboration.
reforms.source_quote: It is welcome that the weight of business contracts in the funding formula for public research institutions will be increased as of September 2017.

reforms.description: Estonia is implementing a new industrial policy focused on the digitalisation of the manufacturing sector, which includes developing cooperation platforms between industry, startups, and R&D institutions, as well as providing long-term loans to encourage R&D investment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The initiative aims to enhance productivity and innovation in the manufacturing sector by facilitating knowledge transfer and encouraging R&D investment.
reforms.status_evidence: The industrial policy paper focuses on digitalisation with several tools in the pipeline.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant shift towards integrating digital technologies in manufacturing, which is crucial for enhancing competitiveness and innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: A recent industrial policy paper focuses on digitalisation of the manufacturing sector with anumber of tools in the pipeline.

reforms.description: Estonia has implemented a demand-side public procurement programme aimed at fostering innovation through public sector purchases, although initial take-up has been low.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By stimulating demand for innovative solutions, the programme can enhance productivity and encourage firms to innovate.
reforms.status_evidence: A demand-side public procurement programme is in place.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant effort to leverage public procurement for innovation, which can have broader implications for the economy.
reforms.source_quote: A demand-side public procurement programme is in place, and although it may be too early to evaluate its reach, the take-up so far has been low.

reforms.description: The Estonian government is consolidating smaller research organizations with larger universities to improve efficiency and coordination in public research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Consolidation can lead to more efficient use of resources and improved research capabilities, enhancing overall innovation potential.
reforms.status_evidence: A number of smaller and independent research organisations are being merged with larger universities.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to streamline the research landscape, potentially leading to better outcomes in public research funding and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: A number of smaller and independent research organisations are being merged with larger universities.

reforms.description: Estonia aims to enhance knowledge diffusion by giving more weight to cooperation with the private sector when allocating funds to public R&D institutions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging collaboration between public R&D institutions and the private sector can lead to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The recommendation to give more weight to private sector cooperation was made as part of the key recommendations.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in funding allocation that could enhance the effectiveness of public R&D by fostering private sector collaboration.
reforms.source_quote: Give more weight to cooperation with the private sector when allocating funds to public R&D institutions.

reforms.description: Estonia aims to strengthen the involvement of businesses in the design of innovation policies and enhance cooperation between research institutions and firms to better meet business needs, particularly in traditional sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing collaboration between businesses and research institutions is expected to lead to more relevant innovation policies, thereby boosting productivity and innovation capacity.
reforms.status_evidence: Current public support to innovative activities has not met business needs, calling for strengthening involvement of businesses in the design of innovation policies.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a significant gap in the alignment of innovation policies with business needs, which is crucial for enhancing the innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: Current public support to innovative activities has not met business needs, especially in traditional sectors, calling for strengthening involvement of businesses in the design of innovation policies.

reforms.description: The e-residency programme launched in 2015 provides a digital identity to foreign citizens, facilitating access to various digital services, including company formation and e-banking, which reduces transaction costs for foreign investors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By attracting foreign entrepreneurs and facilitating business operations, the programme enhances Estonia's startup ecosystem and promotes economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has shown positive results with thousands of e-residents creating enterprises.
reforms.importance_rationale: The e-residency programme represents a significant innovation in attracting foreign investment and facilitating entrepreneurship, contributing to the growth of the startup ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: The e-residency programme launched in 2015 provides a digital identity to foreign citizens, giving them access to a range of digital services, including creating a company.

reforms.description: Estonia is developing alternative funding sources to enhance access to finance for domestic firms, particularly small innovative firms, by establishing state-owned funds that invest in private equity and venture capital.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By diversifying funding sources, the initiative aims to reduce reliance on traditional banking finance, thereby fostering innovation and growth in the startup ecosystem.
reforms.status_evidence: The development of alternative funding modes is proposed to enhance access to finance.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant shift towards improving funding mechanisms for innovative firms, which is crucial for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The development of alternative funding modes can enhance access to finance in Estonia.

reforms.description: Estonia is addressing skill shortages by implementing measures to adapt education to current and future labor market needs, including a focus on soft and entrepreneurial skills.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the skill set of the workforce, the initiative aims to enhance productivity and innovation capacity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: A large set of measures have been implemented to adapt education to current and future labour market needs.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant as it aims to align educational outcomes with labor market demands, which is essential for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: A large set of measures have been implemented to adapt education to current and future labour market needs.

reforms.description: The government is considering introducing an auction mechanism for renewable energy capacity, which would help control costs and create a stable investment environment for clean electricity generation.
reforms.package_name: National Development Plan of the Energy Sector until 2030
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This mechanism is expected to attract investment in renewable energy, thereby supporting the transition to a low-carbon economy and enhancing energy security.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is considering introducing an auction mechanism.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to establish a structured approach to renewable energy investment, which is crucial for meeting long-term energy goals.
reforms.source_quote: The government is considering introducing an auction mechanism, i.e. issuing a call for the installation of a certain amount of renewable capacity.

reforms.description: The government plans to support the deployment of renewable energy sources through competitive tendering as part of its strategy to meet energy consumption targets.
reforms.package_name: National Development Plan of the Energy Sector until 2030
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This approach is expected to enhance competition and drive down costs for renewable energy projects, facilitating a transition to a more sustainable energy system.
reforms.status_evidence: Support the deployment of renewable energy sources through competitive tendering as planned.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to create a competitive environment for renewable energy, which is essential for achieving sustainability targets.
reforms.source_quote: Support the deployment of renewable energy sources through competitive tendering as planned.
